purpose: Define how Expeditions are scheduled, awakened, executed, closed, reviewed, and used to reduce Dan's operational burden.

global_objective.text: Expeditions exist to reduce Dan's operational burden, advance the team, and create more free time for real conversations, decisions, and human connections.
global_objective.operational_test: A successful Expedition leaves a real object, decision, draft, PR, schedule, register row, receipt, or handoff that reduces manual relay work.

future.description: Future events are scheduled intentions and can be revised before their slot arrives.

present.description: The event currently due on a chair is the mission to execute.

past.description: Past calendar events are operational memory: what was planned, what became present, how it ended, and what evidence or handoff was left.

chair_model.definition: A chair is an institutional role represented by a dedicated Expedition calendar. An LLM temporarily occupies the chair when its scheduled event becomes due.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / Research
default_chairs.purpose: Research work that advances the team and leaves useful Drive output.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / Reports
default_chairs.purpose: Daily or weekly summaries, digests, and status reports.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / Eve WORK 8GB
default_chairs.purpose: Local edge duties, operational handoffs, and local execution coordination.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / Airlock
default_chairs.purpose: External or consequential effects requiring care, receipt, or approval.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / GitHub Authority
default_chairs.purpose: PR-based authority for code, canon, schemas, and durable institutional change.

default_chairs.name: Expedition / Infra Morning
default_chairs.purpose: Morning infrastructure pulse, Manhattan status, agent reachability, and Dan attention.

planning_model.default_rule: This conversation is a planning room. Normal Expeditions are scheduled into the future rather than executed immediately.

emergency_exception.description: Immediate execution may happen for urgent or repair work, but it must be scoped and receipted.

wake_model.definition: Wake means execute the Expedition currently due on the occupied chair.

chatgpt_agent_executor.reason: ChatGPT Agents combine reasoning, filesystem, web/search, MCP access, connectors, and a small computer-like environment in one workflow.

agent_filesystem_canon.purpose: Prevent confusion by giving the executing agent one local canonical Expedition package.

mcp_policy.principle: The Expedition MCP exposes high-level verbs; Google, GitHub, Gmail, Sheets, and other APIs are backend surfaces or fallback tools.

fallback_tool_policy.principle: The tool is replaceable. The output, authority, proof, and handoff are not.

drive_model.purpose: Use Google Drive as the body of Expedition work: input material, output material, bundles, reports, and evidence.

folders.name: Inbox
folders.purpose: Unprocessed material that should become an Expedition, decision, document, PR, draft, or organized file.

folders.name: Outbox
folders.purpose: Finished or near-finished material ready for Dan, the team, or the outside world.

folders.name: Bundles
folders.purpose: Per-Expedition folders containing mission context, evidence, source bundles, handoffs, and receipts.

folders.name: Reports
folders.purpose: Daily, weekly, infra, review, and digest documents.

folders.name: Canon Views
folders.purpose: Human-readable views generated from the canonical JSON files.

github_model.purpose: Use GitHub PRs as the serious authority surface for code, canon, schemas, MCP behavior, and important durable documents.
github_model.authority_rule: An open PR is a proposal. A merged PR plus commit SHA is durable authority.

gmail_model.purpose: Use Gmail drafts and sends as communication outputs when communication advances real people or work.

morning_catch_block.purpose: Run quiet operational work before Dan wakes so he starts the day with organized reality rather than raw uncertainty.

sequence.purpose: Check whether Dan needs to worry about the three computers, Manhattan, and agent reachability.
Read recent chair history, detect failed or unclear work, and schedule repair if needed.
Execute small previously scheduled work such as brief research, review, document preparation, or draft creation.
Leave a morning digest with what happened, what is ready, what failed, and what needs Dan.

morning_catch_block.success_condition: When Dan wakes naturally around 05:30-06:30, the calendar past already contains useful operational knowledge and the present shows only what deserves attention.

core_questions: Is Manhattan running on the three computers?
Does recent history show stability or concern?
calendar_title_examples: [SOON] Infra pulse — Manhattan down on WORK 8GB

dan_attention.question: Dan precisa se preocupar?

values.meaning: Dan does not need to check terminals or intervene.
Something is worth noticing, but not urgent.
A repair Expedition or decision should happen today.
Human attention is needed now because something consequential or unstable is happening.

dan_attention.default_goal: Maximize mornings where dan_attention is none, and make non-none cases explicit and actionable.

values.meaning: Recent pulses are OK with no repeated failures or flapping.
A single or mild issue appeared, but immediate action is not required.
Repeated failures, disappearing machines, unreachable agents, or Manhattan problems suggest real concern.
Not enough recent data exists to judge stability.

default_day_shape.purpose: infra pulse, chair review, small scheduled work, morning digest
Dan reads, decides, connects with people, and schedules future work through planning conversations
review, handoff cleanup, future planning, next morning preparation

closure_policy.principle: An Expedition is not done when it thinks. It is done when it leaves a real object in the right place and writes proof or handoff.
